title: melaleuca capitata
intro: Melaleuca capitata, also known as the cajeput tree or white tea tree, is a species of plant in the myrtle family (Myrtaceae) native to Australia and Southeast Asia. The essential oil extracted from its leaves has been used for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce fever, and ease respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Melaleuca capitata have been used to treat various health conditions, including colds, coughs, skin irritations, and infections, and its health benefits include promoting relaxation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Melaleuca capitata are often distilled to create a tea or used in aromatherapy to promote overall well-being.
